# Religion within the Bounds of Bare Reason

*[Kant](../philosophers/kant.md) · 1793*

A work where Kant interprets religion in the light of moral reason alone and defends the thesis of a "radical evil", a propensity in human nature to subordinate the moral law to self-love. It reduces the essential of faith to what serves morality.
